items.title: There's 'a shock coming' for Tasmanian Labor, party elders warn

children.content: Ahead of a state conference, a former Tasmanian Labor premier and treasurer lash the party's performance, telling the ABC the opposition's policies are indistinguishable from the Liberals — and there's no pathway to government without crossbench support.

gallery.altText: Josh Willie highlighted in red in front of parliament house while David Bartlett and Michael Aird are behind him.

thumbnail.altText: Josh Willie highlighted in red in front of parliament house while David Bartlett and Michael Aird are behind him.

avatar.alt: A man with light green glasses smiles.

items.title: Shark danger from rotting whale forces ocean swim event to move

children.content: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

image.altText: a pink and grey whale carcass in white and aqua waves

items.title: 'People assume we're not real': MPs open up about mental health challenges

children.content: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

image.altText: A composite image using headshots of Ella Haddad, Jeremy Rockliff and Ruth Forrest.

items.title: With most of it not fit to sell, this op shop fixes donated items to reduce landfill

children.content: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

gallery.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

thumbnail.altText: A woman looking at clothes on a rack in an op shop

avatar.alt: A woman with long, brown hair in  pink jumper.

items.title: 'They were already out of their seats': The moment Artemis II astronauts knew they were home safe

children.content: Nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds, a journey deeper into space than ever before and a rapt Earth watching on. This is how four astronauts made history.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in orange NASA-branded space suits pose for the camera with a wave and a thumbs up

items.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it

children.content: It was "the best Canberra scoop in years", but the journalist's own editors did not believe it, and almost buried the story.

image.altText: Old Parliament House, Canberra.

items.title: In the Gulf, states are 'paying the price' for a war they didn't start

children.content: When the Iran war ends, Gulf nations will not only have to repair the economic and physical damage caused by Iran's retaliatory campaign, but will also have to learn to live with a regime that analysts say is "less predictable and more violent than it was two months ago".

image.altText: Missiles produced by Iran's armed forces.

items.title: I thought I understood China. Then I ordered a craft beer

children.content: On a midsummer night last year, I took a sip of lager at a craft beer pub in my hometown in China's south. But this was not just a beer. It was a cultural symbol that shows just how much China's Gen Z is changing.

gallery.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

thumbnail.altText: graphic of two schooners of beer against a red background decorated with Chinese symbols

items.title: Queensland e-bike users say commute time could 'roughly double' under new laws

children.content: E-bike advocates say new speed limits, set to come into effect from July, go against current guidelines and could create unsafe situations.

image.altText: A woman in a black top and bike helmet

items.title: The nannasphere is an antidote to the world's grief and horrors

children.content: Surely one of the best ways to counter the hate, grief and horrors of the world is to foster communities driven by decency, calm and care. Enter: the nannasphere.

image.altText: A group of women wearing bright yellow, hold signs like 'defend the right to protest' outside the NSW Supreme Court.

avatar.alt: A close up photo of Julia Baird, who hair bronde hair and is wearing a navy blazer and blue shirt

items.title: Life as a second-generation human cannonball

children.content: David Smith grew up watching his father being shot out of a cannon, so when the time came, he said it was inevitable he would follow in his dad's flight path.

image.altText: David "The Bullet" Smith being shot out of a cannon at the 2026 Sydney Royal Easter Show.

gallery.altText: A man is shot out of a smoking cannon in a spray of golden sparks.

thumbnail.altText: A man is shot out of a smoking cannon in a spray of golden sparks.

items.title: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me

children.content: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

image.altText: Tom Gleeson on the set of Hard Quiz gesturing behind him with a serious expression.

items.title: 'I sound like a woman now': Why Katy Steele is reimagining her past

children.content: The Little Birdy frontwoman is getting in touch with her old self on her new album and tour.

image.altText: Portrait photograph against a red background showing Katy Steele wearing a sparkling jacket, softly lit

items.title: The 90-day swim that united New Zealand behind ocean conservation

children.content: Jono Ridler swam 1,367 kilometres down New Zealand's east coast to raise awareness for ocean health and to end bottom trawling. His exploits have galvanised a nation.

gallery.altText: Jono Ridler swims in glassy ocean waters
Jono Ridler swims as seen from above
Jono Ridler swims past a lighthouse

items.title: From No Pokies to no MPs: Last SA Best member proud of party's legacy after losing seat

children.content: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

image.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair standing with a cut-out of a man wearing an orange vest

items.title: Kennedy breaks 10 seconds again to claim first national title in men's 100m

children.content: Lachlan Kennedy storms to victory in the men's 100m final at the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney, equalling his personal best of 9.96 seconds.

image.altText: Sprinter Lachlan Kennedy has both fists pumped and a satisfied look on his face after winning the 100m final

items.title: Visa warning as migrant confronts 'horrible situation'

children.content: A Swedish migrant says he fears being banned from Australia after his former employer allegedly mishandled his visa application.

image.altText: Shabbo wears a dark shirt and poses for a photo in a backyard.

items.title: One million NSW commuters to benefit from upgrade to Opal card network

children.content: Concession cardholders and pensioners will soon be able to use credit and debit cards on public transport across New South Wales.

image.altText: a man walks through the opal ticket machines at sydney's opal railway station

items.title: 'John Cena meets Jesus' in bold new exhibition by disabled artists

children.content: Paper dolls, wrestlers and cloudscapes appear in an exhibition by a Western Sydney artist collective at Bathurst Regional Art Gallery.

gallery.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.

thumbnail.altText: Dress the Depressed by Leanne Larwood featuring a life-size picture of a woman surrounded by different clothing items.

items.title: Regional Australia leads GP bulk-billing surge

children.content: A new report shows regional and remote towns are rapidly lifting their GP bulk-billing rates while out-of-pocket costs rise across the country.

image.altText: generic photo of psychologist's hand holding a pen and notepad with a patient

items.title: Hawks end Bulldogs unbeaten run in brilliant Gather Round performance

children.content: Hawthorn delivers a brilliant Gather Round performance to end the Bulldogs unbeaten run, after the Swans beat the Suns at Norwood Oval.

image.altText: A Hawthorn AFL forward pumps his fist in celebration after a goal.

items.title: 'May as well have gone to the pub': Bellamy questions Storm after fourth loss

children.content: The Warriors have ended their decade-long run of outs against Melbourne in style as the Storm officially hit rock bottom following a heavy loss.

image.altText: Melbourne slump together after conceding a try

items.title: Hollingsworth 'moving on' from DQ then reinstatement as national champion

children.content: Claudia Hollingsworth insists she ran a fair race, and wants to move on from the drama surrounding the women's 1,500 metres final which saw her disqualified then reinstated, as Jess Hull crashed out.

image.altText: Claudia Hollingsworth has a distressed look on her face as she runs towards the finish

items.title: Motorsport's 20yo trailblazer driving change with all-female team

children.content: Twenty-year-old Imogen Radburn is leading Australia's first all-female car racing team after uncovering a legion of women desperate to be part of motorsport.

gallery.altText: Imogen Radburn sits in her race car and holds her hands in a love heart shape.

thumbnail.altText: Imogen Radburn sits in her race car and holds her hands in a love heart shape.

items.title: Great-grandfather named Qld's second-oldest newly admitted lawyer

children.content: A 77-year-old great-grandfather from Toowoomba proves age is no barrier to launching a new career as he is admitted as a lawyer.

image.altText: Man wearing a suit and holding a certificate.

items.title: Stolen car used by Sydney men in police chase linked to 'number of shootings'

children.content: The occupants of the allegedly stolen Audi threw fuel jerry cans and bleach containers from the car while trying to evade police, officers say.

image.altText: nsw police officers on a highway standing next to a crashed car that was involved in a police chase

items.title: Maila may be just a tropical low by the time it reaches Australia

children.content: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

image.altText: A satellite image of cyclone Maila off the coast of far north Queensland.

items.title: Australians less likely to get bypass surgery now than 20 years ago

children.content: John Ross may be Australia's longest surviving double bypass patient. Here's how has the cardiac operation has evolved over the past 50 years.

image.altText: A black and white photo of a surgical operation.

items.title: Thai fishing port highlights 'big trouble' caused by Middle East war

children.content: Tens of thousands of small and commercial fishing vessels in Thailand are stranded on shore, no longer able to afford diesel, which has more than doubled in price since the Iran war began.

articles.title: 'It's a cutthroat business': Where Australia is sourcing oil right now

children.content: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

image.altText: Graphic of Australia with shipping routes and a oil tanker vessel sailing in the sea.

articles.title: Iran's grip on Strait of Hormuz 'not part of our planning', Israel ambassador says

children.content: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.
